canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, XWinograd is consistently described as a multilingual or cross-lingual benchmark designed to test pronoun resolution. It is based on the Winograd Schema Challenge and is categorized as a "discriminative task". The provided papers frame what this task measures in slightly different ways. One definition presents it as a benchmark for "commonsense reasoning", and it is used in practice to measure the construct of `Commonsense understanding`. Another definition characterizes it as a task for testing "coreference and syntactic understanding", which aligns with its use in measuring `Coreference resolution`. The common element across these descriptions is the focus on pronoun resolution in diverse languages as the core evaluation method.
papers: AfroCS-xs: Creating a Compact, High-Quality, Human-Validated Code-Switched Dataset forAfrican Languages
Your Model is Overconfident, and Other Lies We Tell Ourselves
Emergent morpho-phonological representations in self-supervised speech models
Leveraging Text-to-Text Transformers as Classifier Chain for Few-Shot Multi-Label Classification

definitions.text: A multilingual benchmark for commonsense reasoning based on the Winograd Schema Challenge, testing pronoun resolution.
definitions.paper: Your Model is Overconfident, and Other Lies We Tell Ourselves

definitions.text: Cross-lingual pronoun resolution task testing coreference and syntactic understanding in diverse languages.
definitions.paper: Leveraging Text-to-Text Transformers as Classifier Chain for Few-Shot Multi-Label Classification
